name: Enrico Caruso
abstract: Enrico Caruso (25 February 1873 – 2 August 1921) was an Italian operatic tenor, who sang to great acclaim at the major opera houses of Europe and the Americas, appearing in a wide variety of roles that ranged from the lyric to the dramatic. Generally recognized as the first international recording star, Caruso made around 250 commerciall…

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Green
about_quotes.evidence: I even heard the famous Irish singer -- what's his name -- and Caruso, Enrico Caruso at the time, you know.

about_quotes.speaker: Schuller Gunther
about_quotes.evidence: Enrico Caruso did not compose, period. He just sang what Verdi wrote.
about_quotes.doc_title: Schuller Gunther — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Whaley
about_quotes.evidence: when Enrico Caruso would come to Boston at the opera house, he'd come down there and Sunday afternoon at 2 o'clock and he'd stand up there and direct -- we'd play all the overtures... he'd give us $200 and divide it amongst the band.
